title: Chapter Forty-Four: Blue Warrior
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The hierarchy of the murim was like the Yangtze River. Upper and lower did not change.

Desolate Sect was different. They called it the Great Ming Empire's sword. They valued sharp blades above seniority, forbade master-disciple bonds, and introduced promotions.

"It's like the Desolate Exam," the Annihilation Team Leader said. A middle-aged man with a skinny build and sunken eye sockets, his black robe hanging loose on a thin frame. No one laughed. His martial prowess rivalled Ma Jin's. "It proceeds in two tests. Discernment and martial power. Other items are substituted by merit records."

The Desolate Sect Lord closed her eyes again after declaring the test's opening.

Those seated in long lines received blank paper. Yeon-shin sat in the middle and spread out his sheet. The brush and ink beside him felt unfamiliar -- he had not held them since the Jeong family's annihilation.

*Cheong Myeong said it was nothing special.*

It was not. Stone tablets, rocks, tree bark -- all lifted up, bearing marks of martial arts techniques. White warriors had to identify which sect's martial art had left each trace. An eye technique test, asking about depth of experience and discernment.

Yeon-shin got three wrong. Out of twenty. The Zhongnan Sect's Heavenly River Thirty-Six Swords and Taiji Formless Sword, and Mount Hua's Plum Blossom Sword he knew from his time at Zhongnan. The rest -- his murim knowledge was simply too narrow.

Cheong Myeong shot arrows with a smiling mouth. Three wrong out of twenty was a failing level, he said. Unless the second test was near perfect.

"It can't be helped," the seniors said, patting his shoulders. "Blue level is reached only after broadening knowledge for at least ten years."

"Since there's no precedent like Lightning Flash."

The Radiant Demon Wing First and Second Teams showed not a trace of worry. They just smiled.

"Go ahead," Hyeon Won-chang said. "I'll follow soon." His expression was full of determination.

*Whatever.*

Yeon-shin smiled slightly. "Seems we'll do the next mission together too."

"How does Sir know that?"

"Just a feeling."

As his upper dantian grew, sometimes a kind of premonition stimulated his mind. Usually they came true. It was not a good feeling. It felt like heaven giving alms before early death.

A junior brought news of his opponent. Shin So-bin -- a girl with impressive clear eyes, the same age as Yeon-shin. Said to be a once-in-a-generation talent following Lightning Flash, the sister of the Desolate Sect's Shin family heir.

"Crimson Day Sword from the Annihilation Team. Not good at all," she said. "He's been a white warrior for twenty years. Quite venomous. They say he uses all sorts of miscellaneous techniques, even making the Lazy Donkey Roll into a killing move. Senior should be careful!"

"Right. Thanks."

Using the Lazy Donkey Roll as a killing move. Rolling one's body on the ground despite humiliation. Not caring about face.

*Desolate Sect has all kinds of martial artists.*

"Senior, are you being cautious? You'll have to catch hidden weapons too."

"I'm always tense."

Yeon-shin moved his steps. The Annihilation Team Leader was already calling Lightning Flash's name.

"Sir Jeong! Think of the celebratory wine!" Hyeon Won-chang shouted from behind. Baek Mi-ryeo's scolding followed -- not to pressure him.

---

The crowd formed a huge circle. Examiners with stern eyes. Whispers rising like ripples.

"Lightning Flash and Crimson Day Sword. The intention behind the match is visible."

"Shining new talent versus seasoned combat techniques."

"The odds?"

"Looking at merit, Lightning Flash. But Crimson Day Sword's accumulated years are thick."

Yeon-shin felt deja vu. It had been like this during the Desolate Exam -- standing in such a place before half a year had passed. Even the fame of White Qilin Namgung Hwa-sin, his opponent then, had not resonated.

*Much was unfamiliar then.*

It was the day he had knocked on the door thinking only that he had to live. Now was no different. The increased gazes and momentum could not dig into his nerves.

Only one unfamiliar middle-aged warrior captured his vision.

"Lightning Flash." Crimson Day Sword's impression was different from expected. Not despicable. A scarred veteran with the energy of a squad leader who had gone through thick and thin. The warrior spoke gravely.

"I've heard your fame. In Desolate Sect we accurately gauge skill and merit. The emergence of an outstanding junior is delightful."

"I've heard much of Senior Crimson Day Sword's name too."

"Thank you." A bitter smile. Then a change of expression. "Therefore I will unleash the true techniques of all my martial arts. There may be much you haven't experienced. You might be bewildered, even angered. Will you endure it?"

"Now it seems we should share friendship through swords."

Before they knew it, his hand was going to the Desolate Sword's hilt. Energy waves exploded from Crimson Day Sword's body.

"Good!"

The Annihilation Team Leader stepped back.

Crimson Day Sword took the initiative. Two daggers from his bosom flew like lightning. Yeon-shin had not expected dagger techniques right away. He stepped forward while drawing his sword.

One ring. Both daggers deflected. He had properly drawn the Radiant Demon technique sword path along with the draw.

Master's realm. The feeling rising through his grip was light.

He took a true step.

The training platform thundered. Dirt dust scattered at the tip of his rising blade. Sunlight gathered from the ground as Jeong Family Dynamic Technique and Fate Defying Scripture's power sprouted together, and the Radiant Sword Style's dim light clusters were unleashed at once.

His footwork step transcended the realm of martial arts.

Overwhelming genius.

He instantly occupied the distance where the opponent could not properly apply force when drawing. He gave his all from the first move -- no dragging it into a battle of fundamental martial power. His upper dantian had already completed its calculations from the moment he saw Crimson Day Sword's technique speed and limb positions when throwing the daggers.

That was enough.

One strike. Sword force transcending white-level sent Crimson Day Sword's hastily drawn blade flying.

"What..."

The Desolate Sword rested at the veteran's neck.

The Annihilation Team Leader's eyes widened. Colourless light nets flashed in them -- a supreme master obsessed with martial arts.

"Not martial arts," the Team Leader breathed. "Innate martial talent. No martial artist of equal rank could possibly handle Lightning Flash. Is this Desolate Sect's blessing? It's my first time envying Ma Jin." His eyes met Yeon-shin's. "You've won, Lightning Flash."

"Thank you."

"There will be no more matches for you. This promotion test is perfect. Crimson Day Sword, prepare for your second."

"Yes, squad leader."

Crimson Day Sword looked at Yeon-shin. Shame in his eyes, perhaps, for having spoken of his martial arts' true techniques. But Yeon-shin paid no mind and made his courtesy. The veteran finally smiled.

"Work hard for the civilians. Your talent can truly reach all under heaven."

Yeon-shin silently raised his clasped fists.

The path back to Radiant Demon Wing was quiet. Only whispers like ripples. The white-level seniors who had shot out momentum at him earlier closed their mouths. He recalled when his sword broke against Namgung Hwa-sin's one strike.

*The passage of generations.*

"How did you do that?" Shin So-bin ran up, nearly grabbing his collar. "What footwork was that? No, what about the sword technique that scattered light? They were all martial arts I've never seen!"

"..."

He lightly pushed away his frantic junior's shoulders.

"You made up for it," Cheong Myeong said.

Yeon-shin slowly nodded.

---

The promotion ceremony was grand. Dancers and musicians performed at the training ground. Such was the meaning of the blue martial uniform -- a symbol announcing one as Desolate Sect's main force.

"May sunlight not fade from your path ahead," the Desolate Sect Lord said.

An elf clan blessing. She personally helped him put on the robe. Her hand movements were delicate. The blue uniform fit -- not borrowed, not oversized. His own.

Desolate Sect blue warrior.

A name that could act imposingly anywhere under heaven.

"Congratulations."

"Youngest and shortest time!"

The Radiant Demon Wing seniors welcomed him. He had become one pillar of the division that was always short-handed, where individual blue-levels were known to match the Nine Sects' elite.

"This way," Do Yu-won guided.

Yeon-shin barely had time to confirm the etiquette he had learned since entering Desolate Sect. He was led with the other promoted warriors to a deep place within the fortress he had not known existed -- a very splendid pavilion.

The expressions of the other blue warriors were not good. Visibly hardened faces. Only Yeon-shin maintained a peaceful appearance.

"He is someone with great changes in his heart," Do Yu-won warned. "Please take utmost care with your attitude."

They had come to meet the crown prince.

There were rumours Yeon-shin had heard in passing. The crown prince's mood was sour because the Desolate Sect Lord -- the Great Ming Empire's grand preceptor and military king -- had directed her attention at Lightning Flash.

*What do they want?*

Yeon-shin was already thinking in an extremely martial artist-like way. He surprised himself with the thought.

---
